response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a skilled full-stack and distributed systems engineer with strong craftsmanship practices, but he does not meet the minimum qualification of 8 years of C++ experience — the resume shows only approximately 6 months of C++ work at National Instruments and a university thesis project, with the primary stack being TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang throughout his career. The role's core technical requirement is deep C++ expertise for a cloud compute platform, and the candidate's cloud architecture exposure is limited to AWS CDK usage at Optilyz without evidence of owning or leading large-scale compute infrastructure. The gap in both C++ depth and cloud platform technical leadership is too substantial for this mid-senior role.
strengths: Proven distributed systems experience building low-latency microservices handling millions of HTTP requests per second at Dunnhumby
Consistent software craftsmanship practices including TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and active community mentoring via workshops and talks
End-to-end platform ownership demonstrated through GISCollective, an open source Kubernetes-deployable geo-data platform maintained over multiple years
concerns: Critical minimum qualification gap: 8 years of C++ required; resume shows only ~6 months at National Instruments plus a thesis project, with no C++ in any subsequent role
No demonstrated technical leadership of cloud infrastructure or DevOps engineering teams at the required seniority level
Cloud architecture experience is shallow — limited to AWS CDK at one employer with no evidence of designing or owning production-scale compute platform architecture
